title: CI: cgroup.freeze flake is back
body:
The nightmareish cgroup.freeze flake [is back](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6214067159302144/html/system_test.log.html):
```
# STEP 6: RUN ln -s /no/such/nonesuch /a/b/c/badsymlink
# error opening file `/sys/fs/cgroup//system.slice/crun-buildah-buildah113061327.scope/container/cgroup.freeze`: No such file or directory
# kill container: No such process
```
This is on Fedora 31, and yes, it has [crun-0.14.1-1](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6214067159302144?command=package_versions#L9)

@giuseppe PTAL. I know this is probably going to need to be fixed in crun but I'm filing here as a way to track future flakes.

comments.body:
we need to revendor buildah to get: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2434

The race is in Buildah that is trying to kill a container that is already exited.
@TomSweeneyRedHat is working on a rebase for podman-1.15 to fix 2.0.* but we could separately declare a new master branch to vendor it in.

Ping, what is the status of this? I see #7203 bringing in a new buildah v1.15.1-0.20200731151214-29f4d01c621c, but the flake continues to happen:


46 podman build - stdin test
* fedora-31 : test fedora-31
  * PR #7369
    * [08-19 06:48](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6106498675769344/html/system_test.log.html)
  * PR #7239
    * [08-10 13:42](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6152505279119360/html/system_test.log.html)
* fedora-32 : test fedora-32
  * PR #7240
    * [08-10 11:51](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4937290520723456/html/system_test.log.html)


42 podman build - basic test
* fedora-32 : test fedora-32
  * PR #7296
    * [08-11 15:43](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5924226425159680/html/system_test.log.html)

Another one today

39 podman build - basic test
* fedora-31 : test fedora-31
  * PR #7356
    * [08-20 05:05](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6633365052850176/html/system_test.log.html)

Someone pretty please, help. This is flaking at least once per day, causing much lost effort. Most recently in the [v2.0.5 build PR](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6530978346696704). @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @giuseppe @mheon anyone, please?
I've been trying to reproduce locally without any luck so far, let's improve the buildah error message and hope it can show something more useful: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2559

Woot, I just reproduced it with podman @ master @ 8fdc1169546421a239644159bbaf38db407f2b0f:
```
2020-08-25T20:28:03.000008576Z: error opening file `/sys/fs/cgroup//user.slice/user-0.slice/user@0.service/crun-buildah-buildah230253409.scope/container/cgroup.freeze`: No such file or directory
2020-08-25T20:28:03.000014193Z: kill container: No such process
error running container: error reading container state: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN apk add nginx": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```
Unfortunately I don't see the 'got output' string.

@giuseppe I hand-patched buildah per your 2559 above. The output is not helpful:
```
2020-08-25T21:19:21.000764686Z: error opening file `/sys/fs/cgroup//user.slice/user-0.slice/user@0.service/crun-buildah-buildah368188798.scope/container/cgroup.freeze`: No such file or directory
2020-08-25T21:19:21.000768720Z: kill container: No such process
error running container: error reading container state (got output: ""): exit status 1
```
What else do you suggest that I could try?

My reproducer (please don't judge me):
```console
# cat foo.sh
#!/bin/bash

set -e

timeout --foreground -v --kill=10 60 ../bin/podman build -t foo - <<EOF
FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest
RUN mkdir /workdir
WORKDIR /workdir
RUN /bin/echo hello
RUN apk add nginx
RUN echo jnycVMjJfiJTIZtnvhgIta9v359dTI7fiYFrWmeNbzg1zu5e6M > /v4LZDGa4woigKduvCVsX
EOF

../bin/podman rmi foo

# chmod 755 foo.sh
# while ./foo.sh;do echo;done
```
On a 1minutetip f32 VM, seems to fail within 15-25 minutes.

Thanks for the hint.  I wasn't yet able to reproduce it on my machine (and in a VM), but inspecting more the code, I've found the root issue of the race: https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/474

It can be easily reproduced by adding a sleep just before crun tries to read the cgroup:

```
diff --git a/src/libcrun/container.c b/src/libcrun/container.c
index b4ffa7b..3efaf93 100644
--- a/src/libcrun/container.c
+++ b/src/libcrun/container.c
@@ -2192,19 +2192,10 @@ libcrun_get_container_state_string (const char *id, libcrun_container_status_t *
       if (cgroup_mode < 0)
         return cgroup_mode;
 
+      sleep (1);
       ret = libcrun_cgroup_is_container_paused (status->cgroup_path, cgroup_mode, &paused, err);
       if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
```

then I get:

```
...
fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.8/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.8/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
(1/2) Installing pcre (8.42-r0)
(2/2) Installing nginx (1.14.2-r2)
Executing nginx-1.14.2-r2.pre-install
Executing busybox-1.28.4-r1.trigger
OK: 6 MiB in 15 packages
2020-08-27T10:13:49.000527147Z: error opening file `/sys/fs/cgroup//system.slice/crun-buildah-buildah129670298.scope/container/cgroup.freeze`: No such file or directory
2020-08-27T10:13:49.000536741Z: open pidfd: No such process
```

the different error open pidfd instead of kill is related to crun supporting pidfd now for killing processes.

I'll backport the patch to the Fedora package as soon as it is merged
